rulings.Case Title: JAMES BONG ENTERPRISES, INC. VS. MARIELLEN BAKER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 27, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that the entire claim for $46,400 is subject to res judicata/collateral estoppel due to small claims judgment. No evidence is provided to show what this amount is comprised of (the amounts claimed for rent; the amounts claimed for parking; the amounts claimed for late fees), and if each element of this claim has been addressed in the small claims action. For example, moving party appears to suggest (no declaration or discovery responses provided to this effect) that $46,440 includes, inter alia, unspecified amounts due for parking fees. While landlord's complaint references parking, there is no allegation as to the nature of the contract for parking and whether it was a lease that was due to expire concurrently with the Office Lease attached to the Complaint. Tenant states that there was "an oral agreement  to rent a parking spot for $300 per month." No further terms of this oral agreement are unspecified. (Baker Declaration, paragraph 7). The small claims judgment does not reference any oral agreements or parking; it relieves the tenant of the balance of the lease. The only lease with a balance presented to the Court is the Office Lease. There is no evidence that there was (a) a lease between the parties for parking, that (2) was addressed in the small claims action or (3) of which the tenant was relieved via small claims judgment. If tenant's assumption is correct and the landlord's claim includes parking fees post December 2020, it is impossible to determine whether the entirety of the Plaintiff's sole cause of action for breach of contract is barred by res judicata/collateral estoppel based on the record before this Court as to the claims in this action and the issues presented/resolved in small claims. Late-filed opposition is stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SEAN ROBERT D. ANGLES, AS TRUSTEE OF THE SEAN VS. ROBERT R. RUIZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 27, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and show its entitlement to fees and costs under CCP 998 and/or 874.010. Late-filed opposition (without leave of Court or explanation filed on the reply due date) is stricken. Moving party failed to support its claim for all its attorney's fees as incurred for the common benefit of the parties and therefore, the court will not exercise its discretion to apportion any of the Defendant's fees to the Plaintiff. Moving party further failed to show that there was a valid enforceable and reasonable CCP 998 offer. The offer attached to the moving papers is for "four hundred thousand dollars ($475,000)," but according to the Defendant, Defendant was willing to accept the sum of $475,000, not the sum of $400,000. Furthermore, moving party fails to explain how paying $475,000 is a better outcome than keeping 50% interest in the property worth $1,200,000 (per Defendant's evidence). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ALBERT LEE VS. ANNIE O'KEEFFE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order Regarding Defendant Annie Okeeffes Special Interrogatory Numbers 36 Through 74 And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 27, 2024 line 4. PLAINTIFF ALBERT LEE'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER REGARDING DEFENDANT ANNIE O'KEEFFE'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NUMBERS 36 THROUGH 74 AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS is DENIED. Plaintiff shall provide complete verified responses to special interrogatories within 5 days of notice of entry of order. The additional interrogatories are warranted due to the scope of the issues in this case and the efficiency of the written discovery over a deposition on the same issues. The Court notes that there have been 5 discovery motions in this case so far. If any further motions are filed, the Court will consider appointing a discovery referee at the parties' expense. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ALBERT LEE VS. ANNIE O'KEEFFE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant Annie Okeeffe And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 27, 2024 line 4.
